title: JavaScript Page Printing Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of window.print() and print CSS rules in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the best way to open the print dialog using JavaScript?
What is the purpose of the @media print CSS rule?
options: To define styles that only apply when a page is printed.
To prevent a page from being printed.
To check if the user has a printer connected.
To set the page size to A4 by default.

questions.question: When designing for print, what should you generally hide?
Does window.print() allow you to select which printer to use directly from JavaScript?
options: No, it only opens the browser's standard Print dialog.
Yes, but only if the user has a specific plugin.

questions.question: How can you specify high contrast (black text on white) for printed pages in CSS?
options: Using @media print { body { color: black; background: white; } }
Using the browser's default print software setting only.
